agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive delivery with a 2-hour window, premium pricing ($12.99 surcharge), and conditional refund policy. Stakeholders have conflicting views on whether the time window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA, and on refund automation vs. manual processing.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, express dispatch, real-time tracking, SLA enforcement, and refund adjudication. Intersects with Driver Management (ratings), Warehouse/Inventory (proximity), and Billing/Finance (refunds).",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target 2-hour delivery window from order placement to delivery completion, available to eligible customers for an additional fee." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects express delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery fee charged for all deliveries regardless of express status. Never refunded under the express SLA policy." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period starting from order placement within which the express delivery is targeted for completion." },
      { "term": "Pickup Deadline", "definition": "The 15-minute window from order placement within which an express order must be picked up by a driver to remain on track for the delivery window." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Customer", "definition": "A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse at the time of order placement." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is eligible to be assigned express deliveries." },
      { "term": "SLA Breach", "definition": "The event when an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering the refund evaluation process." },
      { "term": "Refund Adjudication", "definition": "The process of determining whether an SLA breach qualifies for an express fee refund, considering exclusion conditions such as incorrect customer address." },
      { "term": "Driver Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period within which a replacement driver must be assigned after a mid-delivery driver cancellation." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A non-contractually-obligated refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed, framed as a customer goodwill gesture rather than a legal entitlement." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — beyond 10km the 2-hour window is logistically impossible. Offering express outside this radius would guarantee SLA breaches and erode trust.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address >10km from all warehouses, the express delivery option must not be selectable."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be adjusted by product/finance leadership.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects express delivery, the order total includes the base delivery fee plus exactly $12.99."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint — if pickup exceeds 15 minutes, the 2-hour delivery window becomes physically unachievable. This is a hard cutoff that protects SLA integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes of placement, the system must escalate (e.g., reassign, alert ops, or downgrade to standard)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Rating threshold is a quality policy that ops leadership could adjust (e.g., to 3.5 or 4.5) based on driver supply.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, that driver must never appear in the express dispatch candidate pool."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an SLA breach occurs (delivery not completed within 2 hours), the express fee is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund scope is a business/finance policy that could be changed (e.g., partial base fee refund, credit instead of refund).",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a completed express delivery where delivery_completed_at - order_placed_at > 2 hours and no exclusion applies, a refund of exactly $12.99 (express fee) is initiated."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Exclusion policy — finance could add or remove exclusion conditions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an SLA breach where the delivery address was flagged as incorrect (e.g., driver-reported, geocoding mismatch), no refund is processed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute replacement window is an operational SLA that could be tuned based on market conditions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an active express delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund is triggered."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express delivery refunds are processed within 3 business days of the SLA breach.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is a finance operations policy, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an approved express refund, the refund transaction must be initiated within 3 business days of breach determination."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express deliveries must provide real-time driver tracking to the customer.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "For a time-critical premium product, real-time visibility is a core product invariant — without it, the customer cannot verify the service they are paying a premium for.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an active express delivery, the customer must see live driver location updates at intervals no greater than 30 seconds."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from a warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — must clarify whether the 10km boundary is inclusive (<=10km) or exclusive (<10km). Recommend inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "No express-qualified driver (4+ stars) is available within 15-minute pickup window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should the order be downgraded to standard with express fee reversed, or should dispatch attempt lower-rated drivers? Must define fallback." },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up at minute 14 but delivery completes at 2h05m", "expected_behavior": "SLA breach applies — refund should be evaluated. Pickup was within window but delivery was not." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes delivery address mid-delivery to a location >10km from warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does address change void express status? Does the wrong-address exclusion apply if customer self-corrects?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels (second cancellation)", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does the 10-minute replacement window reset? Is there a max reassignment count before auto-refund?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at 1h55m into delivery (5 minutes before window expires)", "expected_behavior": "10-minute replacement window would expire after the 2-hour delivery window. Auto-refund should trigger immediately since SLA breach is inevitable." },
    { "scenario": "SLA breach occurs but customer also gave a wrong address", "expected_behavior": "Per BR-6, no refund — wrong address exclusion overrides the SLA breach refund. But what if address was only partially wrong (e.g., wrong apartment number, correct building)?" },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed moments before warehouse closes", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — no mention of warehouse operating hours or express availability windows." },
    { "scenario": "GPS/tracking system goes down during an express delivery", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does loss of tracking constitute a service failure? Does the customer get a refund if they couldn't track?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer is within 10km at order time but road distance far exceeds 10km (e.g., river, highway topology)", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — is the 10km threshold straight-line (geodesic) or driving distance? Major impact on eligibility accuracy." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream on premium-willing customers. However, SLA breach refunds are a direct margin hit — if breach rate exceeds ~15-20%, the product becomes unprofitable after accounting for operational overhead (dedicated high-rated drivers, faster dispatch). The 3-business-day refund window provides some cash flow buffer but creates reconciliation complexity.",
    "operational": "Significant operational pressure: 15-minute pickup SLA requires pre-positioned drivers or warehouse-proximate driver pools. 4-star driver requirement constrains the eligible driver pool — in markets with few high-rated drivers, express may be functionally unavailable. 10-minute replacement window requires real-time dispatch monitoring and automated reassignment. This is a fundamentally different operational model from standard delivery.",
    "user": "Strong value proposition for time-sensitive customers willing to pay premium. Real-time tracking and prominent 2-hour window set clear expectations. However, the legal 'best effort' framing directly conflicts with the PM's 'guarantee' language — if marketed as guaranteed but terms say best-effort, customer trust erodes on first breach. The refund-as-goodwill framing means customers have no recourse if refund is denied.",
    "risk": "HIGH — the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is a reputational and potential regulatory risk (false advertising in some jurisdictions). Automatic refund triggers (driver cancellation + 10min window) could be gamed by bad actors. Wrong-address exclusion requires robust verification to avoid wrongful refund denials and customer complaints.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Premium option for faster delivery; risk of broken expectations if 'guarantee' language is used but not legally backed." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "4-star minimum creates a two-tier system; express-qualified drivers may face higher pressure and stricter time constraints. Non-qualified drivers lose access to premium orders." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA requires orders to be pick-and-pack ready almost immediately — may require dedicated express lanes or pre-staging." },
      { "group": "Dispatch/Ops Team", "impact": "Real-time monitoring of express orders, automated escalation for pickup breaches, driver cancellation replacement — significantly higher operational load." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue stream but refund processing burden; need to track SLA breaches, adjudicate exclusions, process within 3 days." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must resolve guarantee vs. best-effort language before launch. Refund policy terms must be clearly communicated to avoid consumer protection issues." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Higher contact volume expected — express customers with unmet expectations will escalate faster. Support needs clear playbook for wrong-address disputes and refund adjudication." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "Spec contains a fundamental contradiction between Product (guaranteed 2-hour delivery) and Legal (best-effort SLA, not contractual). This is not a minor wording issue — it changes the entire product promise, refund obligation model, and marketing language. Additionally, Customer Success describes automatic refunds on driver cancellation while Finance describes a 3-business-day manual process, creating a second contradiction on refund timing/automation. Multiple critical gaps remain (distance calculation method, fallback behavior, operating hours, replacement escalation policy). Cannot define reliable acceptance criteria until the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is resolved at the executive level.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) is consistently stated",
      "10km warehouse proximity requirement is clear",
      "15-minute pickup window is operationally justified",
      "4-star driver minimum is a clear eligibility threshold",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded — consistent across sources",
      "Wrong-address exclusion for refunds is clear from Finance"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — fundamental product identity question",
      "Whether refunds are automatic (Customer Success) or manually processed within 3 business days (Finance)",
      "What happens when no express-qualified driver is available",
      "How 'wrong address' is defined and verified",
      "Whether 10km is geodesic or driving distance",
      "Express availability hours and warehouse operating constraints"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time",
      "Driver rating is calculated at assignment time (not a lifetime average that could change mid-delivery)",
      "A single warehouse serves as the origin for express orders (not multi-warehouse routing)",
      "The 10-minute driver replacement window starts at the moment of driver cancellation"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Product Manager describes the 2-hour delivery as 'guaranteed' while Legal explicitly states it is a 'best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This is a foundational contradiction that affects product positioning, marketing language, refund obligation logic, and customer expectations. The system cannot encode both — either the refund is an obligation triggered by SLA breach, or it is discretionary goodwill.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Product", "Marketing", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Frame as guarantee with legally-binding refund obligation — higher customer trust, higher financial exposure, requires Legal sign-off",
        "B: Frame as best-effort target with discretionary goodwill refund — lower risk, but weaker product proposition and customer trust",
        "C: Hybrid — market as 'Express Promise' (not guarantee), with automatic refund as standard practice but legal terms preserving discretion"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — use 'Express Promise' language that signals strong commitment without legal guarantee. Implement automatic refunds as the default path (meeting customer expectations) while preserving exclusion conditions (wrong address) and discretionary override in terms of service. This requires Product, Legal, and Finance to align on exact language."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success states refunds should be 'automatic' when a replacement driver isn't assigned within 10 minutes. Finance states refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These cannot both be true — either the refund is triggered and issued immediately (automatic) or it enters a queue and is processed within 3 business days (manual/batch). The system design is fundamentally different for each.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Support", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Fully automatic — refund is initiated instantly upon trigger (SLA breach or replacement timeout), processed same-day",
        "B: Auto-approved, batch-processed — trigger automatically approves the refund, but actual payment processing happens in nightly/daily batch within 3 business days",
        "C: Manual adjudication — all breaches enter a review queue, processed within 3 business days"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — auto-approve refunds on trigger (SLA breach, replacement timeout) so the customer immediately sees 'refund approved,' but actual payment processing follows Finance's 3-business-day cycle. This satisfies Customer Success's 'automatic' requirement while respecting Finance's processing timeline."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 10km eligibility radius is undefined in measurement method — geodesic (straight-line) distance vs. driving distance can differ by 30-100% in urban areas with rivers, highways, or one-way systems. This directly affects which customers see the express option and whether the 2-hour window is achievable.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Geodesic distance — simpler to compute, but may include customers who are actually 15-20km by road",
        "B: Driving distance — more accurate but requires routing API calls at eligibility check time, adding latency and cost",
        "C: Geodesic with conservative buffer (e.g., 7km geodesic ≈ 10km driving) — simple and safe"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B (driving distance) for accuracy, with result caching per delivery zone. If latency is a concern at launch, Option C as an interim measure."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No fallback behavior is defined for when the 15-minute pickup window or driver availability constraints cannot be met. What happens to the order? Is it auto-downgraded to standard? Is the express fee reversed? Is the customer notified and given a choice? This is a critical operational flow with no specification.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-downgrade to standard delivery with express fee reversal and customer notification",
        "B: Hold order and keep trying for express driver, extending the pickup window",
        "C: Cancel order entirely and notify customer"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — auto-downgrade to standard with immediate express fee reversal and proactive customer notification. Holding the order (B) delays delivery further; cancellation (C) is hostile."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No definition of what constitutes a 'wrong address' for purposes of the refund exclusion (BR-6). Is it driver-reported? System-detected via geocoding? Does a minor error (wrong apartment number vs. wrong city) still trigger exclusion? Who adjudicates disputes? This exclusion will be a major source of customer complaints if not precisely defined.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Legal"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver-reported wrong address, subject to customer dispute process",
        "B: System-verified via geocoding mismatch (address doesn't resolve to a deliverable location)",
        "C: Tiered — minor errors (apt number) don't exclude refund; major errors (wrong street/city) do"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C with Option B as the detection mechanism — use geocoding to classify severity, and only exclude refunds for addresses that resolve to a fundamentally different location. Minor corrections should not penalize customers."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot proceed to acceptance criteria — the core product promise (guarantee vs. best-effort) is contradicted between Product and Legal.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-5 (refund on SLA breach) cannot be correctly specified until the nature of the SLA is resolved." },
    { "violation": "Refund automation model is contradicted between Customer Success (automatic) and Finance (3 business days).", "business_rule_broken": "BR-7 (auto-refund on replacement timeout) and BR-8 (3-day processing) cannot both be true as stated." }
  ]
}
